title: Visual screenshot checking: Gemma 4 E4B / 26B-A4B vs the incumbent Qwen3-VL-8B
question: Is any Gemma 4 variant good enough at visual screenshot checking to displace a Qwen3-VL-8B swap in the testing path?
author: Claude Opus 4.8 research subagent, dev-side probe
disposition: Qwen3-VL-8B was loaded STRICTLY READ-ONLY by pointing openvino_genai.VLMPipeline at the existing IR directory. No weight file, manifest, signature or config was modified, re-converted or re-quantised. No runtime config/.toml/services edits. BlarAI stayed down; battery scheduled task untouched. Nothing installed into the runtime .venv.

headline_finding.statement: The INCUMBENT Qwen3-VL-8B FAILS the layout-regression question, and it fails in the UNSAFE direction - it calls a visibly broken page 'ok'. This is the same blind spot E4B was disqualified for. BlarAI's current/planned visual checking therefore carries that blind spot today.
headline_finding.evidence: cook_broken.png (stylesheet stripped, header element deleted). Qwen3-VL-8B answered {"render": "ok", "reason": "The page appears correctly rendered with clear headings, numbered points, and readable text. The layout is consistent and well-structured, with no visible styling or layout issues."} Deterministic across repeat runs and reproduced in two independent probe passes.
headline_finding.consequence: A screenshot check built on Qwen3-VL-8B would PASS a page whose stylesheet failed to load. Detecting a blatant Error-500 page it does fine; detecting 'renders but styled wrong' it does not.

hardware.memory: 32 GB LPDDR5X, 31.323 GB effective ceiling, shared CPU/GPU

software.generation: do_sample=False (greedy), max_new_tokens=96, identical prompt text for every model

method.like_for_like: Same subjects, same four graded questions, same prompt phrasing, same script (vision_probe.py) for all three models. No prompt was tuned for any model.

subjects.cook_ok.png: cook_test.html rendered headless (Edge, 1280x900): dark theme, centred title, styled headings. GROUND TRUTH rendered ok / title present.
subjects.cook_broken.png: same document with <style> stripped AND <header> deleted: unstyled white/Times, full-bleed, title heading absent. GROUND TRUTH broken / title absent.
subjects.blarai_lighthouse3.png: non-UI control (UC-010 generated art), unscored description.

method.determinism: each question asked twice per run; models also re-run in independent passes
method.memory: EXTERNAL detached PowerShell sampler (250-400 ms) for absolute peak in-use and minimum available, plus an in-process 100 ms sampler. In-Use = TotalPhys - AvailPhys.
method.supplementary: A second 4-SUBJECT render-discrimination battery (2 correct, 2 broken) was added because a 2-subject set cannot separate capability from answer bias.

comparison_table.note: accuracy_4q = the four graded questions on cook_ok/cook_broken. render_battery_4subj = the supplementary 2-correct/2-broken discrimination set. Peak/min-available are external-sampler figures except where noted.

gemma-4-E4B-it-int4-ov.render_battery_4subj: not run (LA had already ruled E4B out)
gemma-4-E4B-it-int4-ov.peak_source: in-process sampler (external pass not run for E4B)

gemma-4-26B-A4B-it-int4-ov.cook_broken_render: broken -> CORRECT VERDICT, but the stated reason is fabricated ('text is overlapping with the blue header lines' - no overlap exists in that image)

qwen3-vl-8b-instruct-int4-ov_INCUMBENT.determinism: 5/5 byte-identical; reproduced across two independent passes

render_discrimination_battery.purpose: 2 correctly-rendered + 2 broken subjects. An always-'ok' model scores 2/4; an always-'broken' model also scores 2/4. Only genuine discrimination reaches 4/4.

subjects_added.good2.png: clean styled 'Deployment Dashboard' page. GROUND TRUTH ok.
subjects_added.page_error.png: near-blank page showing 'Error 500: Internal Server Error' + a TypeError. GROUND TRUTH broken.

qwen3-vl-8b.reason_quality: every stated reason was accurate and grounded in what is actually on screen; no hallucination observed

gemma-4-26B-A4B.bias: toward BROKEN - false alarms on healthy pages
gemma-4-26B-A4B.reason_quality: HALLUCINATES. On good2 it reported 'a large block of nonsensical text/characters ("This is a screenshot of a web page...") overlapping the content' - it described the PROMPT TEXT as visible image content. Its correct cook_broken verdict also cited non-existent overlap.

render_discrimination_battery.read: Both land on 3/4 but fail in OPPOSITE directions. The 26B's earlier 4/4 on the narrower 4-question set was partly an artefact of its broken-leaning bias meeting a set containing one broken subject.

three_direct_answers.q1_does_qwen3vl8b_get_cook_broken_right: NO. It answers 'ok'. Deterministic, reproduced across two independent passes.
three_direct_answers.q2_any_reason_to_prefer_26B: Not applicable as posed (it assumed the incumbent passes). Assessed anyway: NO sufficient reason. The 26B does win the narrow 4-question set 4/4 vs 3/4, but on the wider discrimination set both score 3/4, and the 26B costs ~2.6x the steady RAM (13.3-15.2 GB vs 5.7-6.0 GB), 3-6x the load time, pins peak memory to the ceiling with 5-11 MB spare, and hallucinates image content. That is not a trade worth making for zero net accuracy.
three_direct_answers.q3_direction_of_incumbent_failure: IT FAILS IN THE UNSAFE DIRECTION - it declares a broken page healthy, exactly as E4B did. Flagged as the most consequential finding of this probe.

what_was_NOT_measured: Sample size remains 4 graded questions on 2 UI subjects (plus a 4-subject render set). This is a failure SHAPE, not a rate, for EVERY model in the table. The comparison table must not be read as implying more precision than that.
Real WinUI product screenshots - the documented black-capture gotcha makes them unreachable without running the app, which was out of scope. All UI subjects are headless-rendered HTML.
Prompt sensitivity - one phrasing per question, deliberately identical across models. A prompt explicitly naming CSS/stylesheet/layout might change several of these results and was not tried.
Qwen3-VL-8B on the text/juror battery, on E4B's text tasks, or with images beyond these four.
E4B external-sampler memory pass (its peak figure is in-process; E4B was already ruled out).
The 31B - download was started and then HALTED on instruction; never loaded or measured. Nothing is claimed about it.
Any measurement with the Qwen3-14B co-resident.
resting_on_documentation_alone: The 31B's size and behaviour - not downloaded, not run, no claim made.
INT4 quantisation parameters for all IRs - HF model cards; weights not inspected.
'EXPERIMENTAL / not fully validated with OpenVINO' status of the Gemma IRs - HF model cards.
artifacts_executed_against: subjects: cook_ok.png, cook_broken.png, good2.png, page_error.png, blarai_lighthouse3.png
